title: Landing page and public web presence for loopctl.com

story.i_want_to: see a clear landing page that explains what loopctl is, how it works, and how to get started
story.so_that: I can evaluate whether loopctl fits my AI development workflow and begin onboarding

rationale: loopctl is currently a headless API with no public-facing web presence. To offer it as a service, the root domain needs a landing page that communicates the value proposition, links to documentation and API reference, and provides a path to sign up. Without this, visitors to loopctl.com hit a JSON redirect — not a viable first impression for a product.

acceptance_criteria.description: New deps added to mix.exs: {:phoenix_live_view, '~> 1.0'}, {:phoenix_html, '~> 4.2'}, {:esbuild, '~> 0.8'}, {:tailwind, '~> 0.2'}. LiveView socket uncommented in endpoint.ex. LoopctlWeb macro module (loopctl_web.ex) extended with html/0, live_view/0, and live_component/0 function clauses following Phoenix 1.8 patterns. Existing controller/0 clause with formats: [:json] is unchanged.

A :browser pipeline added to router.ex with: plug :accepts, ['html']; plug :fetch_session; plug :fetch_live_flash; plug :put_root_layout, {LoopctlWeb.Layouts, :root}; plug :protect_from_forgery; plug :put_secure_browser_headers. The GET / route uses the :browser pipeline (live '/'. LandingLive). The GET /health route remains in a separate scope with the :api pipeline. No API routes are affected.

Tailwind CSS v4 asset pipeline configured: assets/css/app.css with @theme block defining design tokens (no tailwind.config.js — v4 uses CSS-only config), assets/js/app.js with phoenix_html import and LiveSocket init. Esbuild and tailwind profiles in config/config.exs (no --config flag for v4). Watchers merged into existing endpoint config in config/dev.exs. Mix aliases added: assets.setup (install binaries) and assets.deploy (minify + phx.digest).

A root layout (root.html.heex) exists with: <html lang='en' class='dark'> (dark mode only — class strategy, not media query), meta tags (charset, viewport, description, og:title, og:description), Geist and Geist Mono font loading via Google Fonts, CSS and JS asset references.

GET / serves an HTML landing page with: hero section (left-aligned, version badge, tagline, two CTAs), features section (icon grid with 6-8 loopctl capabilities), code example section (curl or MCP tool call), and a final CTA. Uses a plain controller + HEEx template (not LiveView) since the page is fully static with zero interactivity — avoids unnecessary WebSocket connections.

Landing page uses Tailwind Plus component patterns adapted to HEEx: hero from hero3 pattern, features from feature2/feature3 patterns, with loopctl design system tokens (slate-*, accent-*) replacing default indigo/gray. Interactive behavior (mobile menu toggle) uses Phoenix.LiveView.JS commands (JS.toggle_class, JS.show, JS.hide) — no Alpine.js or custom JS frameworks.

Landing page includes a sticky navigation bar with: loopctl logo (monospace), links to Features (#features), Docs, API Reference, GitHub, and a Get Started CTA. Mobile nav collapses behind a hamburger button using Phoenix.LiveView.JS for toggle — no custom JavaScript hook needed.
Landing page includes a footer with: copyright, GitHub link, API status link (/health — returns raw JSON when clicked, which is acceptable), and legal placeholder links (terms, privacy).
Endpoint render_errors config updated to: formats: [json: LoopctlWeb.ErrorJSON, html: LoopctlWeb.ErrorHTML]. An ErrorHTML module exists with 404.html.heex and 500.html.heex templates.
The existing /health endpoint and /api/v1/* routes continue to function identically. No regression in API behavior. The root GET / route moves from the :api scope to the :browser scope.

The page is responsive: readable on mobile (320px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px+). CTA buttons have minimum 44px touch targets on mobile (py-3 or min-h-[44px]). Content maxes at max-w-5xl (1024px). Tailwind responsive classes (sm:, md:, lg:) are used for breakpoints.
All text meets WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text) against the slate-950 background. Focus indicators use ring-2 ring-accent-500.

test_cases.name: GET / returns HTML landing page with 200 status
expected_results: Body contains 'loopctl' in a heading element
Body contains navigation links (Features, Docs, API, GitHub)

test_cases.name: API routes still return JSON and are unaffected by browser pipeline
expected_results: /health returns 200 with JSON body containing status: ok

test_cases.name: Landing page contains all required sections
expected_results: HTML contains an element with id='features'
HTML contains a <footer> element with copyright text
HTML contains a <pre> or <code> block (API example)
HTML contains responsive classes (sm: or lg: prefixes in class attrs)

test_cases.name: Root layout includes meta tags, fonts, and dark mode
expected_results: HTML contains a <link> to Google Fonts for Geist
HTML contains a <link> referencing app CSS asset

test_cases.name: 404 page returns HTML for unmatched browser routes
expected_results: Body contains user-friendly error text (not raw JSON)

technical_notes: CRITICAL: Extend lib/loopctl_web.ex with def html, def live_view, and def live_component clauses following Phoenix 1.8 generator patterns. Do NOT modify the existing def controller clause (formats: [:json]) — it serves all API controllers

CRITICAL: Tailwind v4 uses CSS-only config (@theme blocks in app.css). Do NOT create tailwind.config.js. Tailwind CLI args must NOT include --config flag. The tailwind profile in config.exs should be: args ~w(--input=css/app.css --output=../priv/static/assets/app.css)
Router restructure: extract GET /health into its own :api scope. Add new :browser scope with live '/', LandingLive (or controller if using dead view). The two scopes must not share a pipeline
Use a plain controller + HEEx template for the landing page (not LiveView) — the page is fully static, no WebSocket needed. If future interactivity is added, convert to LiveView then

Mix aliases to add: 'assets.setup': ['tailwind.install --if-missing', 'esbuild.install --if-missing'], 'assets.deploy': ['tailwind default --minify', 'esbuild loopctl --minify', 'phx.digest']. Update setup alias to include assets.setup
Dark mode: <html> must have class='dark' unconditionally. Tailwind v4 dark mode uses class strategy by default
Mobile hamburger menu: use Phoenix.LiveView.JS commands (JS.toggle/2, JS.show/2, JS.hide/2) for menu toggle. No custom JS hooks, no Alpine.js
Tailwind Plus HTML → HEEx conversion: replace class with class, use {[...]} syntax for conditional classes. Reference files: docs/tailwind/hero3.html, feature2.html, feature3.html
Config/dev.exs: merge watchers into the EXISTING endpoint config block (do not add a second config block — it would silently override the first)
Update endpoint render_errors: add html: LoopctlWeb.ErrorHTML alongside existing json: LoopctlWeb.ErrorJSON
Design system: docs/design-system.md — Geist fonts, cool slate palette, deep indigo-blue accent, dark mode only
